text: Developing a mobile app involves several important decisions. First, you need responsive design to ensure your app looks good and works well on different screen sizes. Next, you must choose between native and cross-platform development. Native apps are built specifically for iOS or Android, offering high performance and access to device features. Cross-platform apps, like those made with React Native or Flutter, use one codebase for both systems. While they save time and money, they may have slight performance issues. Finally, you submit your app to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store, where users can download it. Balancing performance with development costs is key to success.
